# CANDIDATE: RSI_DIVERGENCE_BULL_BEAR_EXIT

**Filed:** 2026-06-21  
**Filer:** chef-nemotron (free-tier autonomous R&D)  
**Type:** exit_change  
**Status:** DRAFT (NEEDS-RATIFICATION per Rule 9)

## Hypothesis
RSI bullish divergence (price making lower low, RSI making higher low) during an active bearish position indicates weakening momentum and potential reversal. Exiting the bear position at the first RSI divergence signal after entry would avoid further losses on reversing days while allowing profitable trends to run until structural invalidation. This tests whether RSI divergence adds predictive value beyond the existing chart-stop and profit-lock exits.

## Mechanism
- For active BEARISH_REJECTION_RIDE_THE_RIBBON positions, monitor the RSI_DIVERGENCE_BULL watcher signal (watcher_name='rsi_divergence_bull_watcher' from watcher-observations.jsonl).
- If an RSI_DIVERGENCE_BULL signal occurs on the same date as the bear entry AND its bar_timestamp_et is AFTER the bear entry bar_timestamp_et, exit the position at the close of that bar.
- If no divergence signal occurs, follow standard exit logic (chart stop, profit-lock, time stop).
- Only the first divergence signal per trade is considered; subsequent signals are ignored.

## OP-20 disclosures

1. **Account-size assumption:** qty=28 requires $25K+; $1K paper ~= 14% headline. For OP-16 anchor replication, we use the exact J quantities (6,20,10,20,10,3,10 contracts) to isolate edge impact. Real account sizing follows risk-rules.md position tiers.

2. **Sample bias:** Using full 16-month SPY 5m dataset (2025-01-01 to 2026-06-16) for watcher signal detection. RSI_DIVERGENCE_BULL watcher has 42 observations over 41 distinct dates (per leaderboard #11). Selection bias risk: watcher signals generated without look-ahead bias (per LESSONS-LEARNED L14 fix). Overfit risk mitigated by testing on unseen J anchor days (which watcher had zero historical coverage for).

3. **Out-of-sample:** NEEDS-OOS (walk-forward held-out window not yet performed). Will split data: IS 2025-01-01 to 2025-09-30, OOS 2025-10-01 to 2026-06-16.

4. **Real-fills:** NEEDS-REAL-FILLS (top 3 J days not yet validated with realistic OPRA simulation). Will use simulator_real.py with cached OPRA fills for 4/29, 5/01, 5/04.

5. **Failure modes:** 
   - Worst day: Exiting on false divergence signal during strong bear trend (e.g., 5/04), leaving runner profit on table.
   - Max drawdown: Requires equity curve analysis; potential increase if exits occur during temporary pullbacks in strong trends.
   - Blow-up scenario: None additional; divergence exit only reduces exposure versus standard exits.

6. **Concentration:** Will disclose top-5 days % of P&L after backtest. Current BEARISH_REJECTION shows concentration risk (per leaderboard notes); divergence exit may alter concentration profile.

## Pre-merge gate
- Gym validators: all 81/81 PASS (v41) for core logic
- Walk-forward: OOS edge_capture >= 0 on J anchors (no regression)
- Real-fills: <±20% diff vs BS-sim on top 3 J days
- Signal timing validation: >70% of divergence signals fire after bear entry on overlapping dates

## Confidence
2 / 10 -- Zero historical validation of RSI divergence as bear exit signal. Watcher exists but only tested as complementary signal (leaderboard #11). Requires Stage-1 backtest to assess timing and P&L impact.

## Pre-existing leaderboard impact
Complements RSI_DIVERGENCE_BULL_WATCHER (rank 11) by adding exit hypothesis. No conflict with existing candidates: modifies exit logic only for BEARISH_REJECTION positions, while leaderboard candidates are primarily watchers, gates, or new trade classes (e.g., ranks 3-10, 12-22). Synergistic with V14E_BEAR_TIME_OF_DAY_GATE (rank 17) and MIDDAY_TRENDLINE_GATE (rank 21) as they affect entry/timing, not exit mechanics.
